meta.issuer_cn: 宇树科技股份有限公司

company.name_cn: 宇树科技股份有限公司
company.headquarters: Building 1, No. 88 Dongliu Road, Xixing Street, Binjiang District, Hangzhou City, Zhejiang Province, China
company.industry: High-performance general-purpose robotics; R&D, manufacturing and sales of humanoid robots, quadruped robots, robot components, and embodied intelligence models

by_product.product: Humanoid Robots (H1 series and other humanoid robot series)
Quadruped Robots (B series industry-grade and Go series consumer-grade)
Robot Components (joint modules, dexterous hands, collaborative robotic arms, perception sensors, etc.)

products.name: H1 Humanoid Robot
products.specs: First general-purpose humanoid robot released in August 2023; features advanced motion performance, human-like movement, impact resistance, robustness and swarm scheduling capabilities

products.name: Humanoid Robot Series (multiple models, excluding wheeled variants)
products.specs: Four series of self-developed humanoid robots; applied in scientific research, application development, education, cultural performance, and intelligent services

products.name: B Series Quadruped Robot
products.specs: Industry-grade; applied in scientific research, industrial inspection, emergency rescue, and intelligent services

products.name: Go Series Quadruped Robot
products.specs: Consumer-grade; widely adopted by universities, research institutions, technology enterprises, and global developers

products.name: Robot Components
products.specs: Includes joint modules, dexterous hands, collaborative robotic arms, perception sensors and other components

shareholders_pre_ipo.name: Junshi Chuangtou (Junshi Venture Investment /钧石创投)
Hechuang Investment (Hechuang Touzi / 合创投资)
Guangyue Investment (Guangyue Touzi / 光越投资)

projects.name: Intelligent Robot Model R&D Project
projects.focus: Research and development of intelligent robot AI models and embodied intelligence algorithms, including reinforcement learning and advanced control technologies

projects.name: Robot Body R&D Project
projects.focus: Research and development of robot hardware bodies, including structural design, actuation systems, and core components for humanoid and quadruped robots

projects.name: New Intelligent Robot Product Development Project
projects.focus: Development of new types of intelligent robot products to enrich the product matrix and expand application scenarios

projects.name: Intelligent Robot Manufacturing Base Construction Project
projects.focus: Construction of a scalable and intelligent manufacturing facility to support large-scale production of high-performance general-purpose robots

key_risks: Technology iteration and competition risk: The general-purpose robotics industry is characterized by rapid technological innovation and fast product iteration cycles. If the company fails to continuously invest in R&D and maintain its technological leadership in areas such as embodied intelligence, motion control, and perception interaction, it may lose its competitive advantage to domestic and international competitors, leading to deterioration in market share and financial performance.

Market and commercialization risk: The humanoid robot and quadruped robot markets are still in an early stage of development. Large-scale commercial applications have not yet been fully realized, and market demand may fall short of expectations. The company's ability to achieve sustainable large-scale commercialization of its products is uncertain, and revenue growth may not be maintained.

Key personnel and talent concentration risk: The company relies heavily on its founder and actual controller Wang Xingxing and a small group of core technical personnel. The loss of key technical or management talent could significantly impact the company's R&D capabilities, operational continuity, and strategic execution.

Overseas market and geopolitical risk: The company has a significant presence in overseas markets and generates meaningful revenue internationally. Changes in trade policies, export controls, tariffs, geopolitical tensions (particularly between China and the US or other Western countries), and technology export restrictions could adversely affect the company's overseas sales, supply chain, and international expansion plans.

Supply chain and component sourcing risk: The company relies on external suppliers for certain key components including chips, sensors, and other electronic components. Supply chain disruptions, component shortages, price increases, or restrictions on sourcing from specific suppliers (e.g., due to export controls or sanctions) could negatively impact production capacity and cost structure.

Intellectual property risk: The general-purpose robotics industry is highly competitive in terms of patents and proprietary technology. The company may face intellectual property disputes, infringement claims, or challenges in defending its own patents, which could result in litigation costs, damage awards, or loss of competitive technology.

Financial performance volatility risk: As a high-tech, R&D-intensive company at a growth stage, the company's financial results may be volatile. Heavy investment in R&D and manufacturing may lead to significant fluctuations in profitability. The STAR Market characteristics include high R&D investment, operational risk, and unstable performance.

Special voting rights and corporate governance risk: The company has established a special voting rights (weighted voting rights) arrangement or similar governance structure. This may result in the actual controller exercising disproportionate influence over major corporate decisions, potentially harming the interests of minority shareholders.

Industry policy and regulatory risk: The robotics and artificial intelligence industries are subject to evolving government policies and regulations in China and internationally. Changes in industrial policies, AI governance regulations, data security laws, or product safety standards could impose additional compliance burdens or restrict the company's business activities.

Product safety and liability risk: High-performance robots operating in close proximity to humans in various environments may cause personal injury or property damage. Product defects, software failures, or operational errors could result in product liability claims, recalls, reputational damage, and regulatory scrutiny.

Scale-up and manufacturing risk: The company needs to rapidly scale up manufacturing capacity to meet growing market demand. Delays in constructing the intelligent robot manufacturing base, production ramp-up challenges, quality control issues at scale, or inefficiencies in the supply chain could affect the company's ability to fulfill orders and meet revenue targets.

Embodied intelligence model development risk: The development of advanced AI-driven embodied intelligence models requires substantial computational resources, large volumes of training data, and significant R&D investment. There is no guarantee that the company's AI model development will achieve the intended performance levels or commercial applications within expected timeframes.
executive_summary:
Unitree Robotics Co., Ltd. is a Hangzhou-based developer and manufacturer of high-performance humanoid robots, quadruped robots, robot components, and embodied intelligence AI models, recognized as one of the world's leading general-purpose robotics companies.

For the nine months ended September 2025, the company reported revenue of approximately $161M (RMB 1.167B), with adjusted net profit excluding non-recurring items of roughly $59M, implying a solid underlying margin for a growth-stage robotics firm. Full-year and gross margin figures were not disclosed in the filing.

Unitree is seeking to list on Shanghai's STAR Market, offering approximately 10% of shares post-IPO (roughly 4 million new shares). Specific fundraising targets and per-share pricing were not disclosed. The company is sponsored by CITIC Securities.

Founder and Chairman Wang Xingxing controls 34.76% of shares and holds 68.78% of voting rights through a weighted voting rights structure. Notable institutional backers include Sequoia China (Hongshan), Source Code Capital, Jingwei China, Tencent, and China Mobile's venture arm, among a large syndicate of over 40 investors.

Proceeds will fund AI model and embodied intelligence R&D, robot hardware development, new product lines, and construction of a large-scale intelligent manufacturing base.

The three biggest risks are: intense technology competition and the risk of losing R&D leadership; slow or uncertain commercial-scale adoption of humanoid robots; and geopolitical and export-control risks threatening international revenue, which represents a meaningful portion of sales.
